message:
fix(sync): clean up RwLock waiter state on cancellation and poison

ReadFuture and WriteFuture now properly abandon their waiter entries
when returning Cancelled or Poisoned errors, not just on Drop. Previously
a cancelled read or write future would leave its waiter ID in the queue
until Drop, which could delay wake propagation to other waiters if the
future was held alive after cancellation (e.g., stored in a select! branch).

The cleanup logic is extracted into abandon_read_waiter and
abandon_write_waiter methods on RwLock to deduplicate between the
poll error paths and Drop implementations. Both methods handle all
three states a waiter can be in at cleanup time:
1. Still queued (remove from queue)
2. Already granted but guard never taken (release the lock, wake next)
3. Counter incremented but never enqueued (decrement counter, wake
   blocked readers/writers if this was the last writer waiter)

WriteFuture's Drop implementation additionally drops the state lock
before waking, matching the pattern used in ReadFuture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
